chunk_summary: Guidance on energy density and texture safety in complementary feeding for infants aged 6-12 months, emphasizing nutrient-rich foods, choking prevention, and age-appropriate textures.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of energy-dense complementary foods for infants aged 6-12 months.
Identify safe food textures to minimize choking risks during complementary feeding.
Learn evidence-based guidelines for introducing complementary foods to infants.
Recognize the role of macronutrient balance in infant growth and development.
key_facts: Infants aged 6-12 months require complementary foods with high energy density to meet their nutritional needs (WHO, 2020).
Foods should be soft, mashed, or finely chopped to prevent choking (AAP, 2020).
Choking hazards include hard, small, or round foods such as nuts, whole grapes, and raw vegetables (AAP, 2020).
UNICEF recommends introducing iron-rich foods, such as fortified cereals and pureed meats, starting at 6 months.
The ICMR-NIN 2024 guidelines emphasize the gradual transition to family foods by 12 months.
reference_quote: "Complementary foods should be energy-dense, nutrient-rich, and age-appropriate in texture to support growth and development in infants aged 6-12 months." (WHO, 2020)
